node.title: Milestone 0: Query-view registry and runtime feature gating
node.summary: Introduce a runtime-gated query-view registry for prism_query-hosted helpers. Expected touch points are the prism_query host wiring in /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/query_runtime.rs and /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/server_surface.rs, capabilities/resource advertising in /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/server_surface.rs, and durable usage logging in the query/MCP log pipeline. Each view descriptor should carry at least name, feature-flag key, stability stage, owner, and handler wiring. Do not add new top-level MCP tools for this plan; new agent-facing surfaces must stay behind prism_query and be explicitly advertised or omitted at runtime.

acceptance.label: Views can be enabled or omitted independently at runtime without adding new top-level MCP tools
prism://capabilities exposes the enabled/disabled query-view surface with enough metadata for an agent to adapt
Query/MCP logs can aggregate invocation count, unique task usage, latency, and result size by view name

plan.title: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.
plan.goal: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.

node.title: Milestone 1: Harden existing query surfaces and docs
node.summary: Before adding new views, tighten the existing baseline. Focus on predictable prism_query host behavior and docs: normalize disabled-feature and bad-argument errors in /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/query_runtime.rs and /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/query_errors.rs, improve actionability of prism_workset in /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/compact_tools/workset.rs, and make session/task continuity survive daemon restarts instead of dropping the current task binding silently. Update /Users/bene/code/prism/crates/prism-mcp/src/server_surface.rs and the API reference surface so future query views have a stable, documented host contract.

acceptance.label: Current prism_query and compact surfaces produce consistent disabled-feature and bad-argument errors
Daemon restart preserves or explicitly restores the active task/session context needed for follow-on work
prism_workset and API reference output are strong enough to serve as the comparison baseline for new views

plan.title: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.
plan.goal: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.

node.title: Milestone 2: Add repoPlaybook() and validationPlan(...)
node.summary: Ship the first low-risk gated query views behind the new registry: repoPlaybook() and validationPlan(...). These should live behind prism_query, be advertised through capabilities only when enabled, and return compact deterministic JSON with explicit provenance. repoPlaybook() should synthesize how work gets done in the repo from docs, CI, build files, and scripts; validationPlan(...) should map a task, path set, or target to the smallest likely validations using existing repo signals rather than opaque heuristics. Update the API reference with usage examples and fallback behavior when the repo lacks enough signal.

acceptance.label: repoPlaybook() returns actionable build, test, lint, format, and gotcha guidance with sources or provenance
validationPlan(...) returns fast and broader checks with explicit why fields and does not require a new MCP tool
Both views are independently gateable, logged by view name, and documented in the API reference

plan.title: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.
plan.goal: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.

node.title: Milestone 3: Add impact(...) and afterEdit(...)
node.summary: Add the second tranche of gated views once the registry and baseline are stable. impact(...) should summarize likely downstream consumers, breakage zones, and recommended checks from existing graph, ownership, and historical signals. afterEdit(...) should consume changed paths, symbols, or task context to suggest the next files, tests, docs, and risk checks that are most likely missing after an edit. Keep scoring explainable: return explicit why fields and compact evidence instead of opaque prose-heavy recommendations.

acceptance.label: impact(...) returns downstream files or targets, risk hints, and recommended checks with explicit why fields
afterEdit(...) turns recent edits or task changes into the next high-value reads, tests, and docs to inspect
Both views stay compact, deterministic, independently gateable, and measurable through registry logging

plan.title: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.
plan.goal: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.

node.title: Milestone 4: Add command memory from explicit observed evidence
node.summary: Implement command memory as a repo workflow memory layer, not shell interception. Merge static repo-playbook command discovery with explicit PRISM-native observed command evidence, such as successful or failed validation commands already captured through test_ran, fix_validated, or a dedicated command-result mutation path if needed. The surface should recommend repo-specific commands with confidence and provenance, suppress generic shell folklore, and make it clear when a command came from docs versus observed success in this repo.

acceptance.label: The command memory design does not rely on hidden shell interception and works from repo signals plus explicit observed evidence
Recommendations include confidence, provenance, and avoid or caveat signals where appropriate
The resulting surface remains gated, logged, and compact enough to be evaluated against actual agent use

plan.title: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.
plan.goal: prism-query-01: add a runtime-gated query-view registry, harden existing PRISM query surfaces, and ship a small measured set of new agent-facing prism_query views with usage logging and pruning discipline.

node.title: Milestone 5: Add adoption review, prune dead views, and promote survivors
node.summary: Close the loop on registry-backed query views. Add or reuse analysis surfaces over query and MCP logs so maintainers can answer which views are exposed, which are used, how often, by how many tasks, with what latency, and whether fallback behavior suggests the view actually helped. Use those measurements to promote a small set of useful views, keep marginal ones experimental, and remove dead or misleading surfaces instead of accumulating permanent API clutter.

acceptance.label: Maintainers can inspect per-view exposure, invocation counts, unique task usage, and latency from durable logs
The plan yields an explicit promote, keep-experimental, or remove decision for each newly added view
Unused or low-value views do not remain permanently exposed by default

node.title: Milestone 0: Query-view registry and runtime feature gating
node.summary: Implementing the query-view registry, capability advertising, and runtime dispatch plumbing for prism_query-hosted views.

acceptance.label: Views can be enabled or omitted independently at runtime without adding new top-level MCP tools
prism://capabilities exposes the enabled/disabled query-view surface with enough metadata for an agent to adapt
Query/MCP logs can aggregate invocation count, unique task usage, latency, and result size by view name
